data.note:
2026-06-14 09:52 - UI direction = 'Clean docs'. One handwritten, offline style.css (~200 lines) — no Tailwind/CDN/build.
- :root design tokens: neutral gray scale + ONE accent; spacing scale; radii; system font stack (ui-sans-serif/-apple-system; ui-monospace for code + frontmatter).
- Light default; dark via prefers-color-scheme (override tokens only).
- Layout: fixed sidebar (folder/page tree) + content column max-width ~70ch; generous line-height; lots of whitespace; subtle 1px borders; minimal shadows.
- Components: collapsible sidebar tree; page header (breadcrumb title + staleness pill + covers chips); frontmatter table; Compiled Truth (emphasized lead); Timeline (clean list); search box + results; backlinks chips; styled markdown (h1–h4, code blocks, inline code, tables, blockquote, lists).
- Staleness pill: fresh=green, stale=amber, unverified=grey. Accent used sparingly (links, active tree item, focus rings).

data.title: Docs + polish: document kb serve; vendored-asset provenance

data.note:
2026-06-14 09:53 - DESIGN TOKENS (authoritative) — minimal/neutral, restraint + craft. All in :root, tweakable.

PRINCIPLE: prefer whitespace + type hierarchy over chrome. No gradients. Decoration earns its place or is cut. Neutrals are a single slightly-cool gray ramp — never pure #000/#fff.

COLOR light:  --bg #fafafa  --surface #ffffff  --text #1a1a1d  --muted #6b7280  --border #e6e7eb  --accent #4f5bd5 (one restrained blue, low-sat) used ONLY for links / active tree item / focus ring.
COLOR dark (prefers-color-scheme): --bg #0f1115  --surface #15171c  --text #e7e7ea  --muted #9aa1ad  --border #262a31  --accent #8b93f0.

SPACE: 4px base — 4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64. Consistent vertical rhythm between markdown blocks.
RADIUS: 6px cards/pills, 4px inputs.
SHADOW: at most one, subtle — 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.05). 1px hairline borders in --border.

TYPE: --font-sans: ui-sans-serif,-apple-system,'Segoe UI',Roboto,Helvetica,Arial; --font-mono: ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,monospace. Body 16px / line-height 1.6; content measure ~68ch. h1 1.6rem, h2 1.3rem, h3 1.05rem (weight 600, line-height 1.25); meta/small 0.85rem; code 0.9em. Tabular-nums for Timeline dates.

INTERACTION: hover = subtle bg tint (not borders); focus-visible = 2px --accent ring (a11y). Active tree item = accent text + faint accent bg.
STALENESS PILL: small (0.75rem), rounded, MUTED (not saturated): fresh=green, stale=amber, unverified=gray.

data.note: 2026-06-14 09:56 - [human] ADD in-page TOC/navigation: when rendering a page, generate a table-of-contents from its headings (## Compiled Truth, ## Timeline, and any sub-headings) as anchor links — e.g. a right-rail outline (collapses on narrow screens) or a compact outline above the content. Headings get id anchors; clicking scrolls to them; highlight the active section on scroll if cheap. This is the in-PAGE TOC; the sidebar tree (site nav across folders/pages) is the separate foundation already built in c04.

Wiki comments (human → agent feedback)
Comment channel: POST /api/comment + comments.jsonl + kb.py comments CLI
Client: text-selection → floating comment button → modal → submit
Agent monitoring: watch-loop support + passive hook + consumption instructions
Init wiring + docs + polish for comments

data.note: 2026-06-14 15:32 - [human] ANCHOR: the comment record must include a precise location: 'page' (the path) AND 'line' (1-based start line in the page's raw markdown), plus optional 'end_line'. Final record: {id, page, line, end_line, section, selected_text, comment, ts, status}. The server just stores what the client sends; T2 computes line.
data.title: Comment channel: POST /api/comment + comments.jsonl + kb.py comments CLI

data.note: 2026-06-14 15:32 - [human] ANCHOR: compute the line number client-side — the page's raw markdown is already available from /api/page ({markdown}); locate the selected_text in it to get the 1-based start line (and end line if multi-line). Send page+line(+end_line)+section+selected_text+comment. Best-effort match (selected_text disambiguates); the agent gets path+line+text together.
data.title: Client: text-selection → floating comment button → modal → submit

data.note: 2026-06-14 15:32 - [human] AGENT UPDATES THE WIKI: a comment may ask for a change. The agent acts by EDITING the .md file directly (no server write endpoint) — the comment is the human-in-the-loop approval. Protocol: open <page> at <line>, apply the change, append a Timeline entry + bump verified_against if appropriate, then 'kb.py comments resolve <id> --note'. The viewer auto-refreshes (see the new live-refresh tick) so the user sees the edit. Document this in the consumption protocol.
data.title: Agent monitoring: watch-loop support + passive hook + consumption instructions
Live auto-refresh: viewer updates when a wiki file changes

data.note: 2026-06-15 07:45 - [human] ONBOARDING: git hooks are NEVER inherited by clone (.git/hooks/ is local). So the post-commit install must be local, idempotent, and safely re-runnable (don't clobber an existing post-commit hook). A new collaborator won't get this hook from cloning — it's installed by kb.py init or self-healed at SessionStart (see T3).
data.title: Post-commit git hook (diff-scoped, soft, non-blocking)

data.note: 2026-06-15 07:45 - [human] SELF-HEALING BOOTSTRAP (add to SessionStart): since .claude/settings.json IS git-tracked (so SessionStart rides along on clone) but the git post-commit hook is NOT, make the SessionStart hook detect+install the missing .git/hooks/post-commit and create .ingest/ if absent. Net: a fresh clone, after the one-time Claude-Code hook-approval prompt, bootstraps the non-inheritable git hook automatically on first session. Keep it idempotent + silent when already present.
data.title: Chat-extraction triggers: PreCompact + SessionEnd + SessionStart catch-up

data.note: 2026-06-15 07:45 - [human] ONBOARDING DOCS: add a 'New collaborator' section (SKILL.md + repo-wiki/INDEX.md) — what's inherited on clone (repo-wiki/, scripts, .claude/settings.json hooks) vs local-only (git post-commit hook, .ingest/.comments state); the one-time Claude-Code hook-approval prompt is expected; either run 'kb.py init' once or just open a session (SessionStart self-heals). State plainly that fully zero-touch isn't possible (git-hook + trust-prompt are hard constraints); 'one approval then automatic' is the target.
data.title: Docs + init idempotency + end-to-end integration

data.note: 2026-06-15 08:57 - [human] MINING FAN-OUT (add to the cold-start mining prompt, step 3): the agent must farm out maximal work to PARALLEL subagents so bootstrap stays fast + non-blocking. Dispatch one subagent per source (docs-migration / chat-backfill / commit-mining / code-orientation), and parallelize WITHIN a source where possible (one per code subsystem, one per chat-session-batch). Each subagent returns PROPOSED pages only (propose-not-apply) — the main agent collects + presents them for review. Keep the two GATES (structure, ingestion scope) in the interactive main thread with the user; only the mining fans out. Reference the 'heavy work off the main thread' guidance in references/activation.md + references/extraction.md (dispatch to subagents, don't mine inline).
data.title: references/bootstrap.md — interactive 2-gate protocol + cold-start mining prompt
